stemHtml: Surface visibility at a destination in the centre of France in August is reported as 1500 metres in BR (mist), with an inversion at 500 feet above aerodrome level and sky clear above. The pilot makes a non-precision approach to runway 27 shortly before sunset with a minimum descent altitude of 600 feet above aerodrome level. At what range would the pilot expect to see the runway and why?

options.a: Less than 1500 metres because the Sun in the pilot’s eyes will reduce the ability to distinguish objects and therefore air-to-ground visibility.
options.b: Less than 1500 metres because the sunlight reflecting from the top of the layer of mist will reduce air-to-ground visibility.
options.c: Less than 1500 metres because the sunlight reflecting from the water droplets will reduce flight visibility.
options.d: More than 1500 metres because the aircraft will still be flying above the mist layer.
